ground_truth.title: ProxMox or Ubuntu or Debian for Windows virtualization?
ground_truth.author: slow-poke
ground_truth.main_content:
I want to install some yet to be determined flavor of Linux to be the main OS on a PC and then run Win7 and Win10 as virtual machines under Linux. Older laptop; SSD is 1TB, and 32GB RAM, 2nd bay for another SSD but I don't think I need to use it. Anyone here doing this and what would you suggest?

Responses from forum members:

Aarknoid: Running proxmox with ubuntu headless and Truenas as main VMs. Ubuntu runs docker which holds Windows 10 docker, plex, media organizers, and torrent client. Windows 10 as a docker container works well for CAD software but getting GPU shared into Ubuntu was involved - only Nvidia is supported.

mickeyf: Using Debian for 15 years with VirtualBox for Windows 7 with no significant issues. Debian focuses on stability with less frequent major updates. Ubuntu is a downstream derivative of Debian.

jcdammeyer: Tried Linux Mint on an older Windows 7 workstation. Works reasonably well with Windows-like desktop. Installed WinBoat to run Windows 10 as tabs on the Linux desktop. GeoMagic ran reasonably well but was slow - tasks taking seconds on Windows took over a minute. USB pass-through was experimental and didn't work for dongles.

djberta: Has several laptops and desktop on Mint, works well. Debian was used at work for backbone systems.

PeterT: Installed Linux Mint on an old ex-Windows 7 laptop. Linux has trouble with Nvidia graphics drivers causing glitches compared to Windows. Older laptops have better odds of Linux compatibility.
with: I'm running proxmox with ubuntu headless and Truenas as my main VM's.
I have used Debian for the past 15(?) years or so, with BeagleBone at work before I retired.
I've tried to install LinuxMint on my older WIN-7 workstation. Works reasonably well and has a desktop that mimics Windows.
The only somewhat involved part was getting my GPU shared into ubuntu, Nvidia is the only one supported atm that i know of so there is that.
GeoMagic ran reasonably well but the system has what was 15 years ago a high end NVIDIA GTX670.
without: Scam Alert. Members are reminded to NOT send money to buy anything.
